name: Smoked Corn Stuffed Pepper
recipeIngredient: 4 ounces Canned chopped green chilies, drained and rinsed
15 ounces Canned black beans, drained and rinsed
2 ounces Monterey Jack cheese with jalapenos (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut a thin slice from the stem end of each pepper; remove seeds and membranes; rinse.
2. Cook peppers 5 minutes in enough boiling water to cover; drain.
3. Season inside of peppers with 1 teaspoon salt and black pepper; set aside.
4. Combine rice, beans, corn, onion, walnuts, chiles, liquid smoke, cumin, and remaining 1/4 teaspoon salt.
5. Spoon 1 cup of rice mixture into each pepper; stand upright in a 13 x 9-inch baking pan.
6. Cover pan with foil; bake at 350 degrees F for 20 minutes.
7. Remove cover; sprinkle peppers with cheese.
8. Cook an additional 5 minutes or until cheese is melted.
Microwave Oven Instructions:
1. Cut a thin slice from the stem end of each pepper; remove seeds and membrane; rinse.
2. Season with 1 teaspoon salt and black pepper; set upside down on a paper towel to drain.
3. Combine rice, corn, onion, walnuts, chiles, liquid smoke, cumin, and 1/4 teaspoon salt in a 13 x 9-inch microwaveable dish.
4. Cook on HIGH for 2 minutes.
5. Spoon 1 cup of mixture into each pepper; place them back in the dish.
6. Pour 1/4-inch water in bottom of dish.
7. Cover with vented plastic wrap; microwave on MEDIUM (50% power) for 7 minutes.
8. Uncover and sprinkle cheese on top of each pepper.
9. Microwave on HIGH for 1 minute.
10. Garnish with jalapeno pepper slices.
